root_motivation: Joe needs to know his mother is okay without surveilling her. Presence is privacy-by-design: it signals *that* she interacted with wintermute (never *what*), and emits a silence alarm only when she hasn't interacted inside her waking-hours window. It is off unless she knowingly enrolls.
user_persona: Joe Yen, running wintermute on a local Linux laptop. His mother runs a companion device. The daemon runs on his device and observes interaction events that already flow on the agorabus — no new capture.

acceptance_criteria.description: With presence enabled, a published `wm.audio.wake` produces exactly one `wm.presence.summon` on the bus.
A `wm.stt.final { transcript: "hello there" }` produces `wm.presence.summon { transcript_len: 11 }` — the daemon logs and emits no substring of the transcript text anywhere.
The daily counter survives a daemon restart: state file written before exit is read correctly on next start.
With zero interactions inside the configured waking-hours window, exactly one `wm.presence.silence` is emitted after the threshold. Subsequent ticks emit none (debounce).
An interaction inside the window suppresses the silence emission for that window.
Outside waking hours, no `wm.presence.silence` is ever emitted (tested with a clock at 03:00).
With presence disabled in config, the daemon subscribes nothing and emits nothing — verified by an empty bus after wake events.
`wm-presence status` prints today's count and last-interaction timestamp.
The daemon applies the self-emitted-topic filter: it does not consume its own `wm.presence.*` events.
systemd unit installs at the consistent bin path `/usr/local/bin/wm-presence` (no cargo-bin drift).
`cargo test` green; `cargo clippy` clean; autobuilder receipts produced.

scope: Emit `wm.presence.summon { ts, transcript_len }` per interaction (character count, never text)
Persist daily interaction count and last_interaction_ts to a state file
Detect silence within waking-hours window and emit one `wm.presence.silence` per window (debounced)
Opt-in gate: no-op unless presence enabled in /etc/wintermute/conf.d/
non_goals: Does NOT inspect transcript content — only character count
Does NOT store or log any words from wm.stt.final payloads
Does NOT implement distress detection (separate PRD)
Does NOT implement off-device transport (wm-reach handles that)
Does NOT implement enrollment UI (family-enroll PRD)
Does NOT track location, camera, microphone audio level, or any other signal
Does NOT support multi-person enrollment in v1 (single presence subject)

additional.no_transcript_text_in_egress: Any code path that reads wm.stt.final must extract only payload.transcript.len() — the string itself must never appear in a published message, log line, or state file

five_whys_trace.q: Why build a presence daemon instead of just calling periodically?
five_whys_trace.a: Calling periodically is itself surveillance — it requires active monitoring. Presence inverts this: events that already exist on the bus are counted passively.

five_whys_trace.q: Why use the agorabus events rather than a new sensor?
five_whys_trace.a: wm.audio.wake and wm.stt.final already flow from the companion fleet. Adding a new sensor would mean new capture, which violates the privacy constraint.

five_whys_trace.q: Why emit silence instead of just surfacing the count?
five_whys_trace.a: The valuable signal is absence — 'no news today'. A count alone doesn't trigger anything. A silence event lets the digest system send a gentle nudge.

five_whys_trace.q: Why make it opt-in at all, rather than always-on for enrolled devices?
five_whys_trace.a: The subject (his mother) must knowingly choose to share presence data. A device with no enrollment should never emit presence signals to any downstream system.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What counts as 'inside the window' when the daemon starts mid-day?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: The silence threshold is checked against whether the current time is inside the waking-hours window AND interactions since window-start are zero. A daemon that starts at 15:00 with a window of 08:00-21:00 has window-start=08:00 for the current calendar day.

ambiguities_resolved.question: How is transcript_len measured for bare wm.audio.wake (no transcript)?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: A bare wake event produces transcript_len: 0 in the wm.presence.summon payload.
